title: heterostemma oblongifolium
intro: Heterostemma oblongifolium, also known as the "Dwarf Milk Vine," is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Heterostemma oblongifolium include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
